title: Tags, Signatures, Verification, and Release Identity · History Brief: Gita Tag Publication Movement
summary: A release tag moved, a lightweight tag shadowed a branch name, signatures use different formats, and an artifact claims a source revision that was never built. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita tag publication movement increment in release reference, signature, and artifact provenance packet. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Fetch and push tags explicitly, detect movement and namespace collisions, handle deletion, and apply immutable-release policy without assuming universal enforcement.
Compare signed commits, tags, merges, and pushes by what is covered, who verifies them, and which server or client policy applies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Tag Publication Movement
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G61 is a theory in a disposable repository. the annotated tag is cryptographically valid but the signer still needs authorization review. The learner must predict verification exposes signed payload, fingerprint, algorithm, and validity independently of policy, run git verify-tag --raw v2.4.0, explain the signature is valid and the key fingerprint maps to the current release-role allowlist, test this changed condition: move the tag to an unsigned commit and require identity plus artifact provenance checks to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve the observed tag object, target ID, transparency evidence, and artifact digest.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Tag Publication Movement contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G61 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the annotated tag is cryptographically valid but the signer still needs authorization review. The learner must predict verification exposes signed payload, fingerprint, algorithm, and validity independently of policy, run git verify-tag --raw v2.4.0, explain the signature is valid and the key fingerprint maps to the current release-role allowlist, test this changed condition: move the tag to an unsigned commit and require identity plus artifact provenance checks to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve the observed tag object, target ID, transparency evidence, and artifact digest. A defensible solution applies Gita Tag Publication Movement, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to release reference, signature, and artifact provenance packet.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 3
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the annotated tag is cryptographically valid but the signer still needs authorization review
git verify-tag --raw v2.4.0
# expected: verification exposes signed payload, fingerprint, algorithm, and validity independently of policy
# observation: the signature is valid and the key fingerprint maps to the current release-role allowlist
# changed-case: move the tag to an unsigned commit and require identity plus artifact provenance checks to fail
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: preserve the observed tag object, target ID, transparency evidence, and artifact digest
# transfer-limit: signature validity alone does not prove key ownership, authorization, build, or delivered bytes
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Build Gita Tag Publication Movement evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-tag-publication-movement-8pn7id" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Tag Publication Movement into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-tag-publication-movement-8pn7id
# scenario-key: trace-8pn7id probe-di7np8 boundary-n7id8p owner-8pn7idx fault-8pn7idz
# before-ref: main=a11ce8p candidate=b00b1id
# hypothesis: the annotated tag is cryptographically valid but the signer still needs authorization review
git verify-tag --raw v2.4.0
# expected: verification exposes signed payload, fingerprint, algorithm, and validity independently of policy
# observation: the signature is valid and the key fingerprint maps to the current release-role allowlist
# changed-case: move the tag to an unsigned commit and require identity plus artifact provenance checks to fail
# after-ref: main=a11ce8p candidate=c0ffeed
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: preserve the observed tag object, target ID, transparency evidence, and artifact digest
# transfer-limit: signature validity alone does not prove key ownership, authorization, build, or delivered bytes
